celebration: The LAST magic-e vowels — u_e and e_e. The wand has now touched every vowel: a, i, o, u, e. MAGIC-E IS COMPLETE. And 'these' unlocks — the first e_e word, and a piece of glue the class has wanted for a long time.

songs.title: Magic E Makes Cube
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(you… you… you…)
Magic E! Magic E!

[Hook]
Cub… CUBE! Tub… TUBE!
Magic E! Magic E!
Cut… CUTE! Us… USE!
The E is magic! (shhh — it's silent!)

[Verse 2]
The U says its NAME! (U! U!)
And the E says… (nothing!)
Every vowel! (a-i-o-u-e!)
Magic E is DONE!

[Verse 3]
C-U-B-E — cube!
M-U-L-E — mule!
T-U-N-E — tune!
(A tune! A tune!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! No magic E!
(you… you… you…)

[Outro]
The E is magic.
The E is silent. (shhh…)
CUBE!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Transformation pairs on the beat (cub→CUBE, cut→CUTE). The '(you… you… you…)' stutters sing the LONG U sound — cube says /juː/, flute says /uː/; sing the /juː/ 'you' on the stutter (phonetic respelling: sung 'you', printed u_e). Verse 2 is the payoff of the whole series — 'Every vowel! Magic E is DONE!' — celebrate a-i-o-u-e complete. Spell-outs uppercase. Fourth and final week of the Magic E series (cake→bike→bone→cube).

songs.title: The Mule and the Flute
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
The mule has a flute!
The mule has a flute!
A tune, a tune! (a tune!)
The mule has a flute!

[Verse 2]
These cubes! These cubes! (tap! tap!)
These cubes are cute!
A tune, a tune! (a tune!)
The cubes make a tune!

[Verse 3]
The flute and the cube! (toot! toot!)
Snake can hop! Sheep can hum!
A tune, a tune! (a tune!)
The mule can make a tune!

[Outro]
The flute… the tune… (shhh…)
A tune?! (a potato!)
(POTATO!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'The mule has a flute.' — a music week, so let the beat swing. 'These cubes' foregrounds the new e_e glue word and the u_e cubes together. Snake and Sheep join the dance in verse 3. Whisper the outro, potato hits loud and 'plays' the last note.

spreads.artNote: A gentle grey mule alone in the spotlight, ears perked, curious. A story friend (not a named cast member) — 'mule' is fully decodable now.
The mule holding a silver flute to its lips with a hoof, eyes hopeful.
A single golden musical note rising in the spotlight, shimmering — the first tune.
One bright wooden cube in the spotlight, a small bell-shape carved on its face — a music cube.
A whole set of coloured music cubes fanned out — the mule has found a treasure.
The mule beaming over the little cubes, cheek resting on a hoof. Delight.
The mule tapping the cubes with the flute — each cube glows and rings a note. Music blooms.
The mule mid-performance, flute up, cubes ringing — Snake coiled and swaying, Sheep bobbing along. A tiny concert.
The flute rests against a single potato that appears to be 'playing' the last note, a music cube balanced on its head. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it. The mule stares.

sentences: The mule can make a tune.
extras: THE MAGIC-E WAND, week 4 (the last): build cub, touch the red e — cub→cube, tub→tube, cut→cute, us→use. The u changes its voice. Then show that the wand works on e too, though e_e is rare: these, eve, Pete, theme.
MAGIC-E COMPLETE CEREMONY: line up the five wand cards a→a_e, i→i_e, o→o_e, u→u_e, e→e_e. The class has now made every vowel say its name. Mark it on the Pattern Tree.
Pattern-card tracing continues: silent e greyed with a halo — trace, whisper nothing at the e.
Moveable alphabet with the special red e: c-u-b-e, m-u-l-e, t-u-n-e, t-h-e-s-e.

teacherFocus: MAGIC-E, WEEK 4 — the LAST one. Wand pairs for u: cub→cube, tub→tube, cut→cute, us→use. Then show the wand also works on e, though e_e is a small family: these, eve, Pete, theme. THE CEREMONY: line up all five magic-e vowels (a_e, i_e, o_e, u_e, e_e) — the class has now made every single vowel say its name. This is a real milestone; mark it on the Pattern Tree. Note the two long-u sounds: cube/mule say /juː/, flute/tune say /uː/ — accept both, they are the same team.
